task_completion.description: Open the garage floor plan in LibreCAD
task_completion.visual_evidence: LibreCAD window is open, displaying a complex architectural drawing (a 2-car garage floor plan) on the canvas.

task_completion.description: Create 'EVIDENCE' and 'MEASUREMENTS' layers with correct colors
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Layer List panel (usually on the right side) shows new layers named 'EVIDENCE' (with a red color indicator) and 'MEASUREMENTS' (with a blue or cyan color indicator).

task_completion.description: Draw a circle for Item 1 on the EVIDENCE layer
task_completion.visual_evidence: A red circle is visible on the drawing canvas. The LibreCAD command line history may show circle creation commands with coordinates 4500, 2500 and radius 50.

task_completion.description: Draw a line for Item 2 on the EVIDENCE layer
task_completion.visual_evidence: A red line segment is visible on the drawing canvas. The command line history may show line creation commands with coordinates from 5000, 3000 to 5600, 3200.

task_completion.description: Add text labels 'E1' and 'E2' on the EVIDENCE layer
task_completion.visual_evidence: Red text 'E1' is placed near the circle, and 'E2' is placed near the line segment on the canvas.

task_completion.description: Draw triangulation lines for Evidence 1 on the MEASUREMENTS layer
task_completion.visual_evidence: Two blue (or cyan) lines originate from the center of the red circle (E1) and extend to two different reference points on the drawing (representing 0,0 and 6000,0).
The LibreCAD window title bar displays 'crime_scene.dxf', or a 'Save As' dialog is visible showing this filename in the /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/ directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreCAD GUI to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with LibreCAD menus, toolbars, or the built-in command line interface within the LibreCAD window.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a python script or terminal command to generate the DXF file directly
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are open running scripts (e.g., using python and ezdxf) to bypass manual drawing in the GUI.

integrity.description: The drawing elements are genuinely drawn in LibreCAD
integrity.visual_evidence: The drawn elements (circle, lines, text) appear integrated into the LibreCAD canvas, potentially showing selection handles, snap points, or appearing in the command history.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The base floorplan is a 2-car garage drawing. The 'EVIDENCE' layer must be Red. The 'MEASUREMENTS' layer should be Blue (or Cyan, due to a known color code mapping issue). Evidence 1 (E1) is a circle at [4500, 2500] with radius 50. Evidence 2 (E2) is a line from [5000, 3000] to [5600, 3200]. Triangulation lines for E1 go from the circle's center [4500, 2500] to [0, 0] and [6000, 0]. The output file must be saved as '/home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/crime_scene.dxf'.
